# Graphs of exponential and logarithmic functions

## Graphs of Exponential and Logarithmic Functions

[Graph](https://www.monash.edu/student-academic-success/mathematics/mathematics-glossary#Graph) In graph theory (Networks), a structure of vertices connected by edges. In coordinate geometry (Functions and Relations), the set of points in the Cartesian plane representing a relation or function (the plotted representation of a function).s of exponential [function](https://www.monash.edu/student-academic-success/mathematics/mathematics-glossary#Function) A mathematical relation that assigns exactly one output value to each input value.s andВ **logarithmic functions** provide a visual insight into their properties, such as growth, decay, and theВ **inverse** relationship between them. Graphs of exponential functions allow us to examine the distinctive curve of [exponential growth](https://www.monash.edu/student-academic-success/mathematics/mathematics-glossary#Exponential-growth) A process where a quantity increases at a rate proportional to its current value. and decay, while graphs of logarithmic functions facilitate analysis of the inverse of exponentials.

### Inverse relationship between exponential and logarithmic functions

The exponential [function](https://www.monash.edu/student-academic-success/mathematics/mathematics-glossary#Function) A mathematical relation that assigns exactly one output value to each input value. and the logarithmic function are inverses of each other, meaning they вҖҳundoвҖҷ each otherвҖҷs operations.

If the exponential function is given byВ f(x)=ax whereВ aвҲҲR+вҲ–{1}, then its inverse is the logarithmic functionВ g(x)=logaвҒЎ(x) whereВ В aвҲҲR+вҲ–{1}. This relationship implies that applying the logarithmic function to the result of an exponential function returns the original input, and vice versa.

For example, ifВ y=ax, then takingВ logaвҒЎ(y) yieldsВ x, becauseВ logaвҒЎ(ax)=x. Similarly, ifВ y=logaвҒЎ(x), thenВ ay=alogaвҒЎ(x)=x.

This inverse relationship is a fundamental property that links these two functions and forms the basis for solving equations involving [exponential growth](https://www.monash.edu/student-academic-success/mathematics/mathematics-glossary#Exponential-growth) A process where a quantity increases at a rate proportional to its current value. or decay and their corresponding logarithmic expressions.
